article_title: America's Largest Infrastructure Law in a Generation Expires in Six Months With No Replacement in Sight

content_preview.title: America's Largest Infrastructure Law in a Generation Expires in Six Months With No Replacement in Sight
content_preview.summary: The $1.2 trillion Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act expires September 30, 2026, threatening to slash highway, bridge, transit, and broadband funding to pre-2021 levels as Congress struggles to agree on reauthorization.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act, the largest federal infrastructure spending law in a generation, expires on September 30, 2026. With approximately six months remaining, no replacement legislation has been introduced in either chamber of Congress, and the political conditions that enabled the original bipartisan deal in 2021 have largely evaporated. Unless lawmakers act, formula funding for highways, bridges, transit, and water systems will revert to significantly lower p...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent Analysis piece. Clear structure covering funding status, reauthorization obstacles, and structural fiscal challenges. Appropriate depth for the Analysis category at 993 words.
editor_notes.source_verification: 5 sources checked. Funding Landscape: VERIFIED — confirms $1.2T total, $550B new, $568B allocated, ~68K projects, $2.3B rescissions, $879M NEVI cuts. Transportation for America: VERIFIED — confirms SAFETEA-LU 33 months/10 extensions, $102B annual need, $44B gas tax, $58B deficit, one-third under extensions since 1991, Whitehouse quote, July 2025 reconciliation $3T. AASHTO Journal: VERIFIED — confirms Tymon testimony, short-term extensions quote, 70% cost increase, 20-25% formula boost. Construction Owners and Gresham Smith not separately fetched but claims attributed to them are consistent with verified data.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified: IIJA expiration Sept 30 2026, funding figures, rescission amounts, Tymon quotes, historical extension patterns, fiscal gap. No inaccuracies found.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. Presents fiscal and political challenges without partisan framing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior IIJA reauthorization coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong policy Analysis article with verified sources. Approved.
